content: We can reframe Self-Refine's iterative single-perspective self-feedback pipeline as an incomplete self-reflection mechanism, and hypothesize that integrating multiple inconsistent perspectives from LLM self-reflection research into Self-Refine's framework will unlock larger quality gains without losing its core advantage of no extra training or labeled data. This hybrid framework would let Self-Refine not only polish initial outputs through iteration, but also catch blind spots that a single self-feedback perspective would consistently miss.
